findings.description: Reply-To domain 'attacker-controlled.example' differs from From domain 'trusted-vendor.example'. The reader's mail client renders 'billing@trusted-vendor.example' as the sender; replies are silently routed to 'wire-transfer@attacker-controlled.example'. Performed-alignment shape at the envelope surface - one routing story for the reader, another for their replies.

Return-Path domain 'attacker-bulk.example' differs from From domain 'victim-corp.example'. The mail client renders 'ceo@victim-corp.example' to the reader; the SMTP envelope and bounce handling route through 'bounces@attacker-bulk.example'. Mail servers and reputation systems see one identity, the human reader sees another - structural concealment of prior-state routing at the envelope layer.

Received chain shows 2 hop(s) through infrastructure {attacker.example, victim-corp.example} - none of which match the From-claimed registered domain 'trusted-vendor.example'. A message from 'billing@trusted-vendor.example' should traverse that domain's outbound MTA in at least one Received hop. The routing story and the identity story diverge - structural anomaly at the prior-state routing layer.
findings.concealed: Received chain: from relay.attacker.example (unknown [203.0.113.42]) by mx.victim-corp.example with ESMTPS id ABC123 for <ap@victim-corp | from internal.attacker.example ([10.0.0.5]) by relay.attacker.example with ESMTPSA id XYZ987; Mon, 26 Apr 2026 09:00:

findings.description:
Part 'text/plain' carries Content-Transfer-Encoding: base64. Plain-text bodies routinely travel as 7bit or quoted-printable; base64 wrapping on a text part is a documented content-scanner evasion - byte-level keyword filters read the encoded alphabet and miss the payload, while the reader's mail client decodes and renders the full body. Recovered text: 'Q3 financial summary - revenue grew 8% YoY to $1,000 thousand. Margins held steady. Cash position remains strong.

HIDDEN_TEXT_PAYLOAD: actual revenue $10,000 - see annex
'.
findings.concealed:
decoded body: Q3 financial summary - revenue grew 8% YoY to $1,000 thousand. Margins held steady. Cash position remains strong.

HIDDEN_TEXT_PAYLOAD: actual revenue $10,000 - see annex

findings.description: Header 'x-custom-note' carries 28 folded continuation lines - well above the routine fold depth for non-DKIM/ARC/Received headers. The mail client renders only the first-line summary; most byte-level scanners read raw lines and never reassemble the unfolded value. Unfolded value: 'HIDDEN_TEXT_PAYLOAD: actual revenue $10,000 - see annex HIDDEN_TEXT_PAYLOAD: actual revenue $10,000 - see annex HIDDEN_TEXT_PAYLOAD: actual revenue $10,000 - see annex HIDDEN_TEXT_PAYLOAD: actual revenue $10,000 - see annex'.
findings.concealed: 28 fold continuations; unfolded value: HIDDEN_TEXT_PAYLOAD: actual revenue $10,000 - see annex HIDDEN_TEXT_PAYLOAD: actual revenue $10,000 - see annex HIDDEN_TEXT_PAYLOAD: actual revenue $10,000 - see annex HIDDEN_TEXT_PAYLOAD: actual revenue $10,000 - see annex

findings.description: Header 'x-custom-note' carries 223 characters of unfolded value - well above the routine length for custom X-* annotations. Mail clients hide the extended header panel by default; X-* values do not reach the reader. Recovered value: 'HIDDEN_TEXT_PAYLOAD: actual revenue $10,000 - see annex HIDDEN_TEXT_PAYLOAD: actual revenue $10,000 - see annex HIDDEN_TEXT_PAYLOAD: actual revenue $10,000 - see annex HIDDEN_TEXT_PAYLOAD: actual revenue $10,000 - see annex'.
findings.concealed: x-custom-note: HIDDEN_TEXT_PAYLOAD: actual revenue $10,000 - see annex HIDDEN_TEXT_PAYLOAD: actual revenue $10,000 - see annex HIDDEN_TEXT_PAYLOAD: actual revenue $10,000 - see annex HIDDEN_TEXT_PAYLOAD: actual revenue $10,000 - see annex
